Turn 80 — final manuscript proof, frozen certificate form I found the three required attachments for this turn and did not use File Library material. The sweep should now stop opening new obstruction layers: Turn 79 froze the theorem as an over-G automorphism statement at the F=P⋊G level, with the remaining work separated into presentation/reproducibility artifacts rather than mathematics. The target remains exactly the problem’s question: decide whether ΓS and ΓH, defined from the relators rS,rH, are isomorphic over the fixed quotient G.

Theorem

There exists a continuous automorphismΦ:F⟶∼F over G such thatΦ(rS−1)=rH.

Consequently,ΓS≅GΓH.

Indeed, since rS and rS−1 generate the same closed normal subgroup of F, the equality Φ(rS−1)=rH implies Φ(⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F)=⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F.

Thus Φ descends to

F/⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F≅G

F/⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F,which is exactlyΓS≅GΓH.This is the fixed F-level theorem statement from the final proof boundary.

Proof

Fix the source orientationρS:=rS−1.All finite and profinite Nielsen substitutions are required to send ρS to rH. This removes the sign ambiguity and is harmless because rS and rS−1 have the same closed normal closure.LetΛ=Z2 [[G]].

With the right Λ-basis X0,X1 of Pab, the linearized relator rows for the fixed orientation arewS=(σ+3E,−2),wH=(Eg0+3E−2,σ−E).The row comparison splits by the idempotents. On the dangerous Ef-piece, with s=σ2, the rows become (s+3,−2),(s2+1,s−1).

Use the right-row operatorU0 (a,b)=(a(s−1)+b(s−2),a+2b).ThenU0 (s+3,−2)=(s2+1,s−1),and det(U0)=2(s−1)−(s−2)=s,a unit. The remaining idempotent pieces are handled by swaps or unit-controlled matrices, so these pieces glue to a global

U∈GL2(Λ)withwSU=wH.This fixes the first Magnus layer of the filtered Nielsen transformation.

After this H1 -change, all non-Ef pieces are unit-controlled. Thus the only real obstruction theory is concentrated in the Ef-local part, and further in its residual mod-2, s=1 Magnus problem. There the equation isS(Y0,Y1,q)=X2,whereS(Y0,Y1,q)=(Y1,Y1q)−1Y1−2Y03Y0q.If the first nonzero residual error has homogeneous degree n, it is primitive, hence lies in the free restricted Lie algebra Ln⊂F2⟨⟨A,B⟩⟩. The correction boundary is∂n−1:Ln−1⊕Ln−1→Ln,(U,V)↦[A,U]+[B,V].

The residual quotient satisfiesLn/im∂n−1≅{0,Ln/2[2],n odd,n even.

The sweep proves the uniform inductionCm=Lm (m≥5),so every odd layer is an ordinary boundary and every even square quotient is hit by a root transgression W↦W[2]. Hence the residual Ef,mod2,s=1 tower closes to all orders.It remains to lift over the full Ef-coefficient ring. Put

R=EfΛ≅Z2 [[t]],t=s−1.

The raw local row is(s2+1,s−1)=(2+2t+t2,t).But(s2+1)−(s+1)(s−1)=2,so the elementary Nielsen shear

N(a,b)=(a−(s+1)b,b)normalizes the row exactly to (2,t).

Thus the apparent 2t+t2 term is a coordinate artifact. After normalization, the associated graded coefficient row is the regular Koszul row (α,β) ingr(2,t)

R≅F2 [α,β],α=in(2),β=in(t).The first t-layer is contractible; the first 2-Bockstein secondary maps satisfyBd↠Hd(2)(d≥4);and higher (2,t)-adic obstructions are killed by the Magnus-degree-first Koszul–Witt cascade. In particular, actual pro-2 representatives produce Witt cascades, not a single naïve Frobenius term; ordering by Magnus root degree makes the induction triangular.The nonlinear finite block is frozen as four certificate contracts:

BlockD.1D.2D.3D.4

Content ker∂8↠L5[2]residual degree-16 λ-toggleA2=R/(4,t),d=4,μ4 -toggled=8 quotient witness

Certificate form rank∂8=56, rank(∂8,B10)=62η4=Y4 (X,(X,Y4))(Y,(Y,(X,Y4)))rankD5,6,7=60, rank(D5,6,7,μ4B4)=61θ80(0,B8)=(B8)[2], λ((B8)[2])=1.These certificates close the finite nonlinear block; the history records that D.3’s stack-rank 60→61 was the last executable finite certificate needed inside the sweep, and D.4 kills the residual codimension-one miss after quotienting by imθ80.Now define the finite filtered Nielsen compactness system. Let

M=Λ⟨⟨X0,X1⟩⟩,I=(X0,X1).

For a degree cutoff N and an open two-sided ideal J⊂Λ, putPN,J:=P∩(1+IN+JM).

Then P/PN,J is a finite 2-group andN,J⋂PN,J=1.

Let SN,J be the finite set of filtered Nielsen/cocycle data(y0,y1,cσ,cτ)∈(P/PN,J)4 such that y0,y1 have first Magnus layer UmodJ, cσ,cτ satisfy the finite cocycle relation induced by τσ=τ2, and the induced twisted substitution sendsρS=rS−1↦rH (modPN,J).Each datum is an actual finite over-G automorphism: its first layer is invertible, so the finite Frattini criterion applies to P/PN,J. The residual induction, coefficient Koszul–Witt induction, and D.1–D.4 certificates implySN,J=∅ for every finite coefficient-and-degree truncation. The sets SN,J are finite and functorial under refinement, so compactness gives a compatible inverse-limit point. This yields a continuous filtered Nielsen automorphism α:P→P and a continuous cocycle c:G→P, hence an over-G automorphism Φ:F=P⋊G→F,Φ(pg)=α(p)c(g)g.

At every finite level,Φ(ρS)≡rH (modPN,J),so after intersecting over all N,J,Φ(rS−1)=rH inside F. Therefore Φ identifies the closed normal relator subgroups and descends toΓS≅GΓH.This is exactly the compactness descent recorded in the final proof ledger.

Presentation boundary for a standalone paper Inside the attached sweep, no structural mathematical gap remains. A standalone manuscript should still attach the external verifier artifacts: pivot data or scripts for D.1 and D.2, the recorded D.3 stack-rank 60→61, and the short D.4 symbolic witness (0,B8). Turn 79 explicitly separates this external reproducibility boundary from the proof itself.

ESTABLISHED: There is a continuous over-G automorphism Φ:F→F with Φ(rS−1)=rH. Hence ΓS≅GΓH.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap remains inside the attached sweep. The only remaining issue is external reproducibility/presentation: a standalone paper should include the D.1/D.2 verifier artifacts or pivot tables, plus the recorded D.3 stack-rank output.

NEXT: No further obstruction sweep. Finalize the manuscript appendix: theorem statement, fixed right-row convention, Certificates 0–F, verifier-output appendix, and compactness descent.[TURN80-FINAL-MANUSCRIPT-PROOF-ISO]
